revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{河川情報 |名称=駅館川 |画像= |水系=<small>[[二級水系|二級河川]] </small>駅館川 |延長=14.10 |標高= |流量=51.09 |観測所= |流...'
revision.text:
{{河川情報 |名称=駅館川 |画像= |水系=[[二級水系|二級河川]]　駅館川 |延長=14.10 |標高= |流量=51.09 |観測所= |流域面積=15.0 |水源= |河口=[[周防灘]]
（大分県宇佐市） |流域=[[大分県]] }} '''駅館川'''（やっかんがわ）は、[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]を流れ、[[周防灘]]に注ぐ[[二級水系]]駅館川水系の本流。 == 概要 == [[由布岳]]や[[日出生台]]などに水源を発する支流を集めた津房川及び恵良川が、宇佐市[[院内町]]の[[宇佐別府道路]][[院内インターチェンジ]]付近で合流して駅館川となり、宇佐市を南から北に縦貫して周防灘に注ぐ。河口付近には[[沖積平野]]である宇佐平野を形成する。 駅館川水系は[[眼鏡橋]]が多いことで知られており、また、支流には[[東椎屋の滝]]、[[西椎屋の滝]]等の名瀑がある。 駅館川という[[難読地名]]の由来について、[[司馬遼太郎]]は、古代、[[日本の古代道路|駅路]]に置かれた駅家（えきか、えっか）が転じて「やっかん」となり、駅館という字が当てられたという説を唱えている。 == 関連項目 == * [[二級水系の一覧 (大分県)]] [[Category:大分県の河川|やつかんかわ]] [[Category:宇佐市|やつかんかわ]] {{Japan-geo-stub}}

revision.comment: テンプレート河川情報（水系等級、種別）、カテゴリ市町村等削除（[[Wikipedia:ウィキプロジェクト 河川#カテゴリ]]）、stub読み
revision.text:
{{河川情報 |名称=駅館川 |画像= |水系等級=[[二級水系]] |水系=駅館川 |種別=[[二級河川]] |延長=14.10 |標高= |流量=51.09 |観測所= |流域面積=15.0 |水源= |河口=[[周防灘]]（大分県宇佐市） |流域=[[大分県]] }} '''駅館川'''（やっかんがわ）は、[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]を流れ、[[周防灘]]に注ぐ[[二級水系]]駅館川水系の本流。
== 概要 == [[由布岳]]や[[日出生台]]などに水源を発する支流を集めた津房川及び恵良川が、宇佐市[[院内町]]の[[宇佐別府道路]][[院内インターチェンジ]]付近で合流して駅館川となり、宇佐市を南から北に縦貫して周防灘に注ぐ。河口付近には[[沖積平野]]である宇佐平野を形成する。
駅館川水系は[[眼鏡橋]]が多いことで知られており、また、支流には[[東椎屋の滝]]、[[西椎屋の滝]]等の名瀑がある。
駅館川という[[難読地名]]の由来について、[[司馬遼太郎]]は、古代、[[日本の古代道路|駅路]]に置かれた駅家（えきか、えっか）が転じて「やっかん」となり、駅館という字が当てられたという説を唱えている。
== 関連項目 == * [[二級水系の一覧 (大分県)]]
{{Japan-geo-stub|やつかんかわ}} [[Category:大分県の河川|やつかんかわ]]

駅館川という[[難読地名]]の由来について、[[司馬遼太郎]]は、古代、[[日本の古代道路|駅路]]に置かれた駅家（えきか、えっか）が転じて「やっかん」となり、駅館という字が当てられたという説を唱えている。

revision.comment: 小説家の説は不要。しかもどの著作に記述があるかも不明。
revision.text:
{{Infobox 河川 |名称=駅館川 |画像=[[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] |画像説明=駅館大橋から下流方面を見る |水系等級=[[二級水系]] |水系=駅館川 |種別=[[二級河川]] |延長=14.10 |標高=-- |流量=51.09 |観測所= |流域面積=15.0 |水源= |河口=[[周防灘]]（大分県宇佐市） |流域=[[大分県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''駅館川'''（やっかんがわ）は、[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]を流れ、[[周防灘]]に注ぐ[[二級水系]]駅館川水系の本流。
== 地理 == [[由布岳]]や[[日出生台]]などに水源を発する支流を集めた津房川及び恵良川が、宇佐市院内町小坂の[[宇佐別府道路]][[院内インターチェンジ]]付近で合流して駅館川となり、宇佐市を南から北に縦貫して周防灘に注ぐ。河口付近には[[沖積平野]]である[[宇佐平野]]を形成する。
駅館川水系は[[眼鏡橋]]が多いことで知られており、また、支流には[[東椎屋の滝]]、[[西椎屋の滝]]等の名瀑がある。
== 関連項目 == * [[大分県の二級水系一覧]]
{{River-stub|pref=大分県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつかんかわ}} [[Category:大分県の河川]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=駅館川 |画像=[[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] |画像説明=駅館大橋から下流方面を見る |水系等級=[[二級水系]] |水系=駅館川 |種別=[[二級河川]] |延長=43<ref>[http://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html 主要事業の紹介 河川・砂防・港湾等] 大分県宇佐土木事務所、2016年6月1日</ref> |標高=-- |流量= |観測所= |流域面積=390<ref>「[http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html おおいた清らかな水環境保全指針]」{{PDFLink|[http://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf 第2章 水環境を取巻く現状と課題]}} 大分県、2006年3月</ref> |水源= |河口=[[周防灘]]（大分県宇佐市） |流域=[[大分県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''駅館川'''（やっかんがわ）は、[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]を流れ、[[周防灘]]に注ぐ[[二級水系]]駅館川水系の本流である。
== 地理 == [[由布岳]]や[[日出生台]]などに水源を発する支流を集めた津房川及び恵良川が、宇佐市院内町小坂の[[宇佐別府道路]][[院内インターチェンジ]]付近で合流して駅館川となり、宇佐市を南から北に縦貫して周防灘に注ぐ。河口付近には[[沖積平野]]である[[宇佐平野]]を形成する。
駅館川水系は[[眼鏡橋]]が多いことで知られており、また、支流には[[東椎屋の滝]]、[[西椎屋の滝]]等の名瀑がある。
天保12年（[[1841年]]）に完成した『太宰管内志』には、「宇佐の駅宿址は宇佐川の傍らにあり故に駅館川といふなり」と記されており、古代の[[駅家]]が近くにあったことが駅館川の名の由来であるとしている。また、[[鎌倉時代]]以前には、宇沙川、菟狭川とも称した<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 44 大分県』[[角川書店]]、1980年1月、p.829</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[大分県の二級水系一覧]]
{{River-stub|pref=大分県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつかんかわ}} [[Category:駅館川水系|*]] [[Category:大分県の河川]] [[Category:宇佐市の地理]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=駅館川 |画像=[[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] |画像説明=駅館大橋から下流方面を見る |水系等級=[[二級水系]] |水系=駅館川 |種別=[[二級河川]] |延長=43<ref>[http://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html 主要事業の紹介 河川・砂防・港湾等] 大分県宇佐土木事務所、2016年6月1日</ref> |標高=-- |流量= |観測所= |流域面積=390<ref>「[http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html おおいた清らかな水環境保全指針]」{{PDFLink|[http://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf 第2章 水環境を取巻く現状と課題]}} 大分県、2006年3月</ref> |水源= |河口=[[周防灘]]（大分県宇佐市） |流域=[[大分県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''駅館川'''（やっかんがわ）は、[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]を流れ、[[周防灘]]に注ぐ[[二級水系]]駅館川水系の本流である。
== 地理 == [[由布岳]]や[[日出生台]]などに水源を発する支流を集めた津房川及び恵良川が、宇佐市院内町小坂の[[宇佐別府道路]][[院内インターチェンジ]]付近で合流して駅館川となり、宇佐市を南から北に縦貫して周防灘に注ぐ。河口付近には[[沖積平野]]である[[宇佐平野]]を形成する。
駅館川水系は[[眼鏡橋]]が多いことで知られており、また、支流には[[東椎屋の滝]]、[[西椎屋の滝]]等の名瀑がある。
天保12年（[[1841年]]）に完成した『太宰管内志』には、「宇佐の駅宿址は宇佐川の傍らにあり故に駅館川といふなり」と記されており、古代の[[駅家]]が近くにあったことが駅館川の名の由来であるとしている。また、[[鎌倉時代]]以前には、宇沙川、菟狭川とも称した<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 44 大分県』[[角川書店]]、1980年1月、p.829</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[大分県の二級水系一覧]]
{{River-stub|pref=大分県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつかんかわ}} [[Category:駅館川水系|*]] [[Category:大分県の二級河川]] [[Category:宇佐市の地理]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=駅館川 |画像=[[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] |画像説明=駅館大橋から下流方面を見る |水系等級=[[二級水系]] |水系=駅館川 |種別=[[二級河川]] |延長=43<ref>[http://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html 主要事業の紹介 河川・砂防・港湾等] 大分県宇佐土木事務所、2016年6月1日</ref> |標高=-- |流量= |観測所= |流域面積=390<ref>「[http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html おおいた清らかな水環境保全指針]」{{PDFLink|[http://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf 第2章 水環境を取巻く現状と課題]}} 大分県、2006年3月</ref> |水源= |河口=[[周防灘]]（大分県宇佐市） |流域=[[大分県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''駅館川'''（やっかんがわ）は、[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]を流れ、[[周防灘]]に注ぐ[[二級水系]]駅館川水系の本流である。
== 地理 == [[由布岳]]や[[日出生台]]などに水源を発する支流を集めた津房川及び恵良川が、宇佐市院内町小坂の[[宇佐別府道路]][[院内インターチェンジ]]付近で合流して駅館川となり、宇佐市を南から北に縦貫して周防灘に注ぐ。河口付近には[[沖積平野]]である[[宇佐平野]]を形成する。
駅館川水系は[[眼鏡橋]]が多いことで知られており、また、支流には[[東椎屋の滝]]、[[西椎屋の滝]]等の名瀑がある。
天保12年（[[1841年]]）に完成した『太宰管内志』には、「宇佐の駅宿址は宇佐川の傍らにあり故に駅館川といふなり」と記されており、宇佐八幡宮に向かう宇佐使いが休んだ古代の[[駅家]]が近くにあったことが駅館川の名の由来であるとしている<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 日本歴史地名大系ジャーナル 第92回 駅館川]</ref>。また、[[鎌倉時代]]以前には、宇沙川、菟狭川とも称した<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 44 大分県』[[角川書店]]、1980年1月、p.829</ref>。

天保12年（[[1841年]]）に完成した『太宰管内志』には、「宇佐の駅宿址は宇佐川の傍らにあり故に駅館川といふなり」と記されており、宇佐八幡宮に向かう宇佐使いが休んだ古代の[[駅家]]が近くにあったことが駅館川の名の由来であるとしている<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 日本歴史地名大系ジャーナル 第92回 駅館川]</ref>。また、[[鎌倉時代]]以前には、宇沙川、菟狭川とも称した<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 44 大分県』[[角川書店]]、1980年1月、p.829</ref>。

== 関連項目 == * [[大分県の二級水系一覧]]
{{River-stub|pref=大分県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつかんかわ}} [[Category:駅館川水系|*]] [[Category:大分県の河川]] [[Category:大分県の二級河川]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=駅館川 |画像=[[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] |画像説明=駅館大橋から下流方面を見る |水系等級=[[二級水系]] |水系=駅館川 |種別=[[二級河川]] |延長=43<ref>[http://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html 主要事業の紹介 河川・砂防・港湾等] 大分県宇佐土木事務所、2016年6月1日</ref> |標高=-- |流量= |観測所= |流域面積=390<ref>「[http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html おおいた清らかな水環境保全指針]」{{PDFLink|[http://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf 第2章 水環境を取巻く現状と課題]}} 大分県、2006年3月</ref> |水源= |河口=[[周防灘]]（大分県宇佐市） |流域=[[大分県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''駅館川'''（やっかんがわ）は、[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]を流れ、[[周防灘]]に注ぐ[[二級水系]]駅館川水系の本流である。
== 地理 == [[由布岳]]や[[日出生台]]などに水源を発する支流を集めた津房川及び恵良川が、宇佐市院内町小坂の[[宇佐別府道路]][[院内インターチェンジ]]付近で合流して駅館川となり、宇佐市を南から北に縦貫して周防灘に注ぐ。河口付近には[[沖積平野]]である[[宇佐平野]]を形成する。
駅館川水系は[[眼鏡橋]]が多いことで知られており、また、支流には[[東椎屋の滝]]、[[西椎屋の滝]]等の名瀑がある。
天保12年（[[1841年]]）に完成した『太宰管内志』には、「宇佐の駅宿址は宇佐川の傍らにあり故に駅館川といふなり」と記されており、宇佐八幡宮に向かう宇佐使いが休んだ古代の[[駅家]]が近くにあったことが駅館川の名の由来であるとしている<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 日本歴史地名大系ジャーナル 第92回 駅館川]</ref>。また、[[鎌倉時代]]以前には、宇沙川、菟狭川とも称した<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 44 大分県』[[角川書店]]、1980年1月、p.829</ref>。
平清経が入水したのは現在の[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]柳ヶ浦地区・[[駅館川]]沖合といわれており、これにちなんで駅館川河口付近に'''小松塚'''と呼ばれる[[五輪塔]]および[[慰霊碑]]が建てられている（「小松」の名は清経の父・重盛が小松殿と通称されたことに由来する）。また、小松塚のたもとにある橋（駅館川河口に最も近い）も'''小松橋'''と名付けられている。

== 関連項目 == * [[大分県の二級水系一覧]]
{{River-stub|pref=大分県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつかんかわ}} [[Category:駅館川水系|*]] [[Category:大分県の河川]] [[Category:大分県の二級河川]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=駅館川 |画像=[[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] |画像説明=駅館大橋から下流方面を見る |水系等級=[[二級水系]] |水系=駅館川 |種別=[[二級河川]] |延長=43<ref>[http://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html 主要事業の紹介 河川・砂防・港湾等] 大分県宇佐土木事務所、2016年6月1日</ref> |標高=-- |流量= |観測所= |流域面積=390<ref>「[http://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html おおいた清らかな水環境保全指針]」{{PDFLink|[http://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf 第2章 水環境を取巻く現状と課題]}} 大分県、2006年3月</ref> |水源= |河口=[[周防灘]]（大分県宇佐市） |流域=[[大分県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''駅館川'''（やっかんがわ）は、[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]を流れ、[[周防灘]]に注ぐ[[二級水系]]駅館川水系の本流である。
== 地理 == [[由布岳]]や[[日出生台]]などに水源を発する支流を集めた津房川及び恵良川が、宇佐市院内町小坂の[[宇佐別府道路]][[院内インターチェンジ]]付近で合流して駅館川となり、宇佐市を南から北に縦貫して周防灘に注ぐ。河口付近には[[沖積平野]]である[[宇佐平野]]を形成する。
駅館川水系は[[眼鏡橋]]が多いことで知られており、また、支流には[[東椎屋の滝]]、[[西椎屋の滝]]等の名瀑がある。
天保12年（[[1841年]]）に完成した『太宰管内志』には、「宇佐の駅宿址は宇佐川の傍らにあり故に駅館川といふなり」と記されており、宇佐八幡宮に向かう宇佐使いが休んだ古代の[[駅家]]が近くにあったことが駅館川の名の由来であるとしている<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 日本歴史地名大系ジャーナル 第92回 駅館川]</ref>。また、[[鎌倉時代]]以前には、宇沙川、菟狭川とも称した<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 44 大分県』[[角川書店]]、1980年1月、p.829</ref>。
平清経が入水したのは現在の[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]柳ヶ浦地区・駅館川沖合といわれており、これにちなんで駅館川河口付近に'''小松塚'''と呼ばれる[[五輪塔]]および[[慰霊碑]]が建てられている（「小松」の名は清経の父・重盛が小松殿と通称されたことに由来する）。また、小松塚のたもとにある橋（駅館川河口に最も近い）も'''小松橋'''と名付けられている。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[大分県の二級水系一覧]] *[[日本の川一覧]] {{River-stub|pref=大分県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつかんかわ}} [[Category:駅館川水系|*]] [[Category:大分県の河川]] [[Category:大分県の二級河川]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=駅館川 |画像=[[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] |画像説明=駅館大橋から下流方面を見る |水系等級=[[二級水系]] |水系=駅館川 |種別=[[二級河川]] |延長=43<ref>[https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html 主要事業の紹介 河川・砂防・港湾等] 大分県宇佐土木事務所、2016年6月1日</ref> |標高=-- |流量= |観測所= |流域面積=390<ref>「[https://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html おおいた清らかな水環境保全指針]」{{PDFLink|[https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf 第2章 水環境を取巻く現状と課題]}} 大分県、2006年3月</ref> |水源= |河口=[[周防灘]]（大分県宇佐市） |流域=[[大分県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''駅館川'''（やっかんがわ）は、[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]を流れ、[[周防灘]]に注ぐ[[二級水系]]駅館川水系の本流である。

== 主な出来事 == * [[2024年]][[8月29日]] - 台風10号接近に伴う[[集中豪雨]]で、駅館川の別府橋観測所で氾濫危険水位を超過。県と気象台は氾濫危険情報を発出した<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=宇佐市を流れる駅館川 氾濫危険情報（9:15） |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>。

revision.text:
{{Infobox River | name = Yakkan River | image = [[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] | caption = Downstream view from Yakkan Bridge | river_system_class = [[Secondary river system]] | river_system = Yakkan River | river_type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 43<ref>[https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html Introduction to Major Projects: Rivers, Erosion Control, Ports, etc.] Oita Prefecture Usa Civil Engineering Office, June 1, 2016</ref> | elevation = -- | discharge = | monitoring_station = | basin_area = 390<ref>''[https://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html Oita Clear Water Environment Conservation Policy]'' {{PDFLink|[https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf Chapter 2: Current Conditions and Challenges Surrounding the Water Environment]}} Oita Prefecture, March 2006</ref> | source = | mouth = [[Suo Sea]] (Usa City, Oita Prefecture) | region = [[Oita Prefecture]] | notes = | references = }}
'''Yakkan River''' (駅館川, ''Yakkan-gawa'') is a river in [[Usa City]], [[Oita Prefecture]], Japan, flowing into the [[Suo Sea]] as the main stream of the [[secondary river system]] Yakkan River.
== Geography ==
The Yakkan River begins where its tributaries, Tsufu River and Era River, which originate from [[Mount Yufu]] and the [[Hiji Plateau]], converge near Kosaka in [[Usa City|Innai Town]], close to the [[Usa-Beppu Road]]'s [[Innai Interchange]]. The river flows from south to north through Usa City, emptying into the Suo Sea. Near the mouth, it forms the alluvial plain known as the [[Usa Plain]].
The Yakkan River system is known for its many [[arch bridges]], and its tributaries include notable waterfalls like [[Higashi Shiiya Falls]] and [[Nishi Shiiya Falls]].
According to the ''Dazai Kannaishi'' (1841), "The Usa station was located beside the Usa River, hence it was called Yakkan River." == Geography ==
The Yakkan River begins where its tributaries, Tsufu River and Era River, which originate from [[Mount Yufu]] and the [[Hiji Plateau]], converge near Kosaka in [[Usa City|Innai Town]], close to the [[Usa-Beppu Road]]'s [[Innai Interchange]]. The river flows from south to north through Usa City, emptying into the Suo Sea. Near the mouth, it forms the alluvial plain known as the [[Usa Plain]].
The Yakkan River system is known for its many [[arch bridges]], and its tributaries include notable waterfalls like [[Higashi Shiiya Falls]] and [[Nishi Shiiya Falls]].

This suggests the name originates from an ancient [[ekiya]] (travel station) where messengers to [[Usa Hachiman Shrine]] rested.<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 Japan Historical Place Names Journal, Vol. 92: Yakkan River]</ref> Before the [[Kamakura Period]], it was also known as Usa River or Tosa River.<ref>Editorial Committee for the ''Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names,'' Vol. 44: Oita Prefecture. A monument called ''Komatsuzuka'' (a [[gorintō]] and memorial stone) stands near the estuary. The name "Komatsu" derives from Kiyotsune's father, Taira no Shigemori, who was called ''Komatsu-dono.'' Additionally, the bridge closest to the Yakkan River mouth is named ''Komatsu Bridge.''
== Notable Events == * [[August 29, 2024]] – [[Typhoon No. 10 of the Reiwa 6 Era]] caused heavy rainfall, leading the Yakkan River to exceed the flood danger level at the Beppu Bridge monitoring station. Authorities issued flood danger information.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=Flood Warning for Yakkan River in Usa City (9:15) |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>

'''Yakkan River''' (駅館川, ''Yakkan-gawa'') is a river in [[Usa City]], [[Oita Prefecture]], Japan, flowing into the [[Suo Sea]] as the main stream of the [[secondary river system]] Yakkan River.
== Geography ==
The Yakkan River begins where its tributaries, Tsufu River and Era River, which originate from [[Mount Yufu]] and the [[Hiji Plateau]], converge near Kosaka in [[Usa City|Innai Town]], close to the [[Usa-Beppu Road]]'s [[Innai Interchange]]. The river flows from south to north through Usa City, emptying into the Suo Sea. Near the mouth, it forms the alluvial plain known as the [[Usa Plain]].
The Yakkan River system is known for its many [[arch bridges]], and its tributaries include notable waterfalls like [[Higashi Shiiya Falls]] and [[Nishi Shiiya Falls]].
According to the ''Dazai Kannaishi'' (1841), "The Usa station was located beside the Usa River, hence it was called Yakkan River." This suggests the name originates from an ancient [[ekiya]] (travel station) where messengers to [[Usa Hachiman Shrine]] rested.<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 Japan Historical Place Names Journal, Vol. 92: Yakkan River]</ref> Before the [[Kamakura Period]], it was also known as Usa River or Tosa River.<ref>Editorial Committee for the ''Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names,'' Vol. 44: Oita Prefecture. Kadokawa Shoten, January 1980, p. 829</ref>
The site where Taira no Kiyotsune drowned is believed to be near the Yakkan River estuary in present-day Yanagigaura, Usa City. A monument called ''Komatsuzuka'' (a [[gorintō]] and memorial stone) stands near the estuary. The name "Komatsu" derives from Kiyotsune's father, Taira no Shigemori, who was called ''Komatsu-dono.'' Additionally, the bridge closest to the Yakkan River mouth is named ''Komatsu Bridge.''
== Notable Events == * [[August 29, 2024]] – [[Typhoon No. 10 of the Reiwa 6 Era]] caused heavy rainfall, leading the Yakkan River to exceed the flood danger level at the Beppu Bridge monitoring station. Authorities issued flood danger information.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=Flood Warning for Yakkan River in Usa City (9:15) |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Secondary River Systems in Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県の二級水系一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Rivers in Japan|ja|日本の川一覧|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox 河川 |名称=駅館川 |画像=[[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] |画像説明=駅館大橋から下流方面を見る |水系等級=[[二級水系]] |水系=駅館川 |種別=[[二級河川]] |延長=43<ref>[https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html 主要事業の紹介 河川・砂防・港湾等] 大分県宇佐土木事務所、2016年6月1日</ref> |標高=-- |流量= |観測所= |流域面積=390<ref>「[https://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html おおいた清らかな水環境保全指針]」{{PDFLink|[https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf 第2章 水環境を取巻く現状と課題]}} 大分県、2006年3月</ref> |水源= |河口=[[周防灘]]（大分県宇佐市） |流域=[[大分県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''駅館川'''（やっかんがわ）は、[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]を流れ、[[周防灘]]に注ぐ[[二級水系]]駅館川水系の本流である。
== 地理 == [[由布岳]]や[[日出生台]]などに水源を発する支流を集めた津房川及び恵良川が、宇佐市院内町小坂の[[宇佐別府道路]][[院内インターチェンジ]]付近で合流して駅館川となり、宇佐市を南から北に縦貫して周防灘に注ぐ。河口付近には[[沖積平野]]である[[宇佐平野]]を形成する。
駅館川水系は[[眼鏡橋]]が多いことで知られており、また、支流には[[東椎屋の滝]]、[[西椎屋の滝]]等の名瀑がある。
天保12年（[[1841年]]）に完成した『太宰管内志』には、「宇佐の駅宿址は宇佐川の傍らにあり故に駅館川といふなり」と記されており、宇佐八幡宮に向かう宇佐使いが休んだ古代の[[駅家]]が近くにあったことが駅館川の名の由来であるとしている<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 日本歴史地名大系ジャーナル 第92回 駅館川]</ref>。また、[[鎌倉時代]]以前には、宇沙川、菟狭川とも称した<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 44 大分県』[[角川書店]]、1980年1月、p.829</ref>。
平清経が入水したのは現在の[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]柳ヶ浦地区・駅館川沖合といわれており、これにちなんで駅館川河口付近に'''小松塚'''と呼ばれる[[五輪塔]]および[[慰霊碑]]が建てられている（「小松」の名は清経の父・重盛が小松殿と通称されたことに由来する）。また、小松塚のたもとにある橋（駅館川河口に最も近い）も'''小松橋'''と名付けられている。
== 主な出来事 == * [[2024年]][[8月29日]] - [[令和6年台風第10号|台風10号]]接近に伴う[[集中豪雨]]で、駅館川の別府橋観測所で氾濫危険水位を超過。県と気象台は氾濫危険情報を発出した<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=宇佐市を流れる駅館川 氾濫危険情報（9:15） |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[大分県の二級水系一覧]] *[[日本の川一覧]] {{River-stub|pref=大分県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつかんかわ}} [[Category:駅館川水系|*]] [[Category:大分県の河川]] [[Category:大分県の二級河川]]

平清経が入水したのは現在の[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]柳ヶ浦地区・駅館川沖合といわれており、これにちなんで駅館川河口付近に'''小松塚'''と呼ばれる[[五輪塔]]および[[慰霊碑]]が建てられている（「小松」の名は清経の父・重盛が小松殿と通称されたことに由来する）。また、小松塚のたもとにある橋（駅館川河口に最も近い）も'''小松橋'''と名付けられている。
== 主な出来事 == * [[2024年]][[8月29日]] - [[令和6年台風第10号|台風10号]]接近に伴う[[集中豪雨]]で、駅館川の別府橋観測所で氾濫危険水位を超過。県と気象台は氾濫危険情報を発出した<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=宇佐市を流れる駅館川 氾濫危険情報（9:15） |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[大分県の二級水系一覧]] *[[日本の川一覧]] {{River-stub|pref=大分県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつかんかわ}} [[Category:駅館川水系|*]] [[Category:大分県の河川]] [[Category:大分県の二級河川]]

The Yakkan River begins where its tributaries, Tsufu River and Era River, which originate from [[:en:Mount Yufu|Mount Yufu]] and the [[Hiji Plateau]], converge near Kosaka in [[Usa City|Innai Town]], close to the [[:en:Usa-Beppu Road|Usa-Beppu Road]]'s [[Innai Interchange]]. The Yakkan River system is known for its many [[:en:arch bridges|arch bridges]], and its tributaries include notable waterfalls like [[Higashi Shiiya Falls]] and [[Nishi Shiiya Falls]].

Kadokawa Shoten, January 1980, p. 829</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yakkan River]][[ja:駅館川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668226]] Wikidata name: Yakkan River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox River | name = Yakkan River | image = [[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] | caption = Downstream view from Yakkan Bridge | river_system_class = [[Secondary river system]] | river_system = Yakkan River | river_type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 43<ref>[https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html Introduction to Major Projects: Rivers, Erosion Control, Ports, etc.] Oita Prefecture Usa Civil Engineering Office, June 1, 2016</ref> | elevation = -- | discharge = | monitoring_station = | basin_area = 390<ref>''[https://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html Oita Clear Water Environment Conservation Policy]'' {{PDFLink|[https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf Chapter 2: Current Conditions and Challenges Surrounding the Water Environment]}} Oita Prefecture, March 2006</ref> | source = | mouth = [[Suo Sea]] (Usa City, Oita Prefecture) | region = [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]] | notes = | references = }}
'''Yakkan River''' (駅館川, ''Yakkan-gawa'') is a river in [[Usa City]], [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Japan, flowing into the [[Suo Sea]] as the main stream of the [[secondary river system]] Yakkan River.
== Geography ==
The Yakkan River begins where its tributaries, Tsufu River and Era River, which originate from [[:en:Mount Yufu|Mount Yufu]] and the [[Hiji Plateau]], converge near Kosaka in [[Usa City|Innai Town]], close to the [[:en:Usa-Beppu Road|Usa-Beppu Road]]'s [[Innai Interchange]]. The river flows from south to north through Usa City, emptying into the Suo Sea. Near the mouth, it forms the alluvial plain known as the [[Usa Plain]].
The Yakkan River system is known for its many [[:en:arch bridges|arch bridges]], and its tributaries include notable waterfalls like [[Higashi Shiiya Falls]] and [[Nishi Shiiya Falls]].
According to the ''Dazai Kannaishi'' (1841), "The Usa station was located beside the Usa River, hence it was called Yakkan River." This suggests the name originates from an ancient [[ekiya]] (travel station) where messengers to [[Usa Hachiman Shrine]] rested.<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 Japan Historical Place Names Journal, Vol. 92: Yakkan River]</ref> Before the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]], it was also known as Usa River or Tosa River.<ref>Editorial Committee for the ''Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names,'' Vol. 44: Oita Prefecture. Kadokawa Shoten, January 1980, p. 829</ref>
The site where Taira no Kiyotsune drowned is believed to be near the Yakkan River estuary in present-day Yanagigaura, Usa City. A monument called ''Komatsuzuka'' (a [[gorintō]] and memorial stone) stands near the estuary. The name "Komatsu" derives from Kiyotsune's father, Taira no Shigemori, who was called ''Komatsu-dono.'' Additionally, the bridge closest to the Yakkan River mouth is named ''Komatsu Bridge.''
== Notable Events == * [[August 29, 2024]] – [[Typhoon No. 10 of the Reiwa 6 Era]] caused heavy rainfall, leading the Yakkan River to exceed the flood danger level at the Beppu Bridge monitoring station. Authorities issued flood danger information.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=Flood Warning for Yakkan River in Usa City (9:15) |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yakkan River]][[ja:駅館川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668226]] Wikidata name: Yakkan River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox River | name = Yakkan River | image = [[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] | caption = Downstream view from Yakkan Bridge | river_system_class = [[Secondary river system]] | river_system = Yakkan River | river_type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 43<ref>[https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html Introduction to Major Projects: Rivers, Erosion Control, Ports, etc.] Oita Prefecture Usa Civil Engineering Office, June 1, 2016</ref> | elevation = -- | discharge = | monitoring_station = | basin_area = 390<ref>''[https://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html Oita Clear Water Environment Conservation Policy]'' {{PDFLink|[https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf Chapter 2: Current Conditions and Challenges Surrounding the Water Environment]}} Oita Prefecture, March 2006</ref> | source = | mouth = [[Suo Sea]] (Usa City, Oita Prefecture) | region = [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]] | notes = | references = }}
'''Yakkan River''' (駅館川, ''Yakkan-gawa'') is a river in [[Usa City]], [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Japan, flowing into the [[Suo Sea]] as the main stream of the [[secondary river system]] Yakkan River.
== Geography ==
The Yakkan River begins where its tributaries, Tsufu River and Era River, which originate from [[:en:Mount Yufu|Mount Yufu]] and the [[Hiji Plateau]], converge near Kosaka in [[Usa City|Innai Town]], close to the [[:en:Usa-Beppu Road|Usa-Beppu Road]]'s [[Innai Interchange]]. The river flows from south to north through Usa City, emptying into the Suo Sea. Near the mouth, it forms the alluvial plain known as the [[Usa Plain]].
The Yakkan River system is known for its many [[:en:arch bridges|arch bridges]], and its tributaries include notable waterfalls like [[Higashi Shiiya Falls]] and [[Nishi Shiiya Falls]].
According to the ''Dazai Kannaishi'' (1841), "The Usa station was located beside the Usa River, hence it was called Yakkan River." This suggests the name originates from an ancient [[ekiya]] (travel station) where messengers to [[Usa Hachiman Shrine]] rested.<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 Japan Historical Place Names Journal, Vol. 92: Yakkan River]</ref> Before the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]], it was also known as Usa River or Tosa River.<ref>Editorial Committee for the ''Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names,'' Vol. 44: Oita Prefecture. Kadokawa Shoten, January 1980, p. 829</ref>
The site where Taira no Kiyotsune drowned is believed to be near the Yakkan River estuary in present-day Yanagigaura, Usa City. A monument called ''Komatsuzuka'' (a [[gorintō]] and memorial stone) stands near the estuary. The name "Komatsu" derives from Kiyotsune's father, Taira no Shigemori, who was called ''Komatsu-dono.'' Additionally, the bridge closest to the Yakkan River mouth is named ''Komatsu Bridge.''
== Notable Events == * [[August 29, 2024]] – [[Typhoon No. 10 of the Reiwa 6 Era]] caused heavy rainfall, leading the Yakkan River to exceed the flood danger level at the Beppu Bridge monitoring station. Authorities issued flood danger information.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=Flood Warning for Yakkan River in Usa City (9:15) |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yakkan River]][[ja:駅館川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668226]] Wikidata name: Yakkan River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox River | name = Yakkan River | image = [[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] | caption = Downstream view from Yakkan Bridge | river_system_class = [[Secondary river system]] | river_system = Yakkan River | river_type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 43<ref>[https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html Introduction to Major Projects: Rivers, Erosion Control, Ports, etc.] Oita Prefecture Usa Civil Engineering Office, June 1, 2016</ref> | elevation = -- | discharge = | monitoring_station = | basin_area = 390<ref>''[https://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html Oita Clear Water Environment Conservation Policy]'' {{PDFLink|[https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf Chapter 2: Current Conditions and Challenges Surrounding the Water Environment]}} Oita Prefecture, March 2006</ref> | source = | mouth = [[Suo Sea]] (Usa City, Oita Prefecture) | region = [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]] | notes = | references = }}
'''Yakkan River''' (駅館川, ''Yakkan-gawa'') is a river in [[Usa City]], [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Japan, flowing into the [[Suo Sea]] as the main stream of the [[secondary river system]] Yakkan River.
== Geography ==
The Yakkan River begins where its tributaries, Tsufu River and Era River, which originate from [[:en:Mount Yufu|Mount Yufu]] and the [[Hiji Plateau]], converge near Kosaka in [[Usa City|Innai Town]], close to the [[:en:Usa-Beppu Road|Usa-Beppu Road]]'s [[Innai Interchange]]. The river flows from south to north through Usa City, emptying into the Suo Sea. Near the mouth, it forms the alluvial plain known as the [[Usa Plain]].
The Yakkan River system is known for its many [[:en:arch bridges|arch bridges]], and its tributaries include notable waterfalls like [[Higashi Shiiya Falls]] and [[Nishi Shiiya Falls]].
According to the ''Dazai Kannaishi'' (1841), "The Usa station was located beside the Usa River, hence it was called Yakkan River." This suggests the name originates from an ancient [[ekiya]] (travel station) where messengers to [[Usa Hachiman Shrine]] rested.<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 Japan Historical Place Names Journal, Vol. 92: Yakkan River]</ref> Before the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]], it was also known as Usa River or Tosa River.<ref>Editorial Committee for the ''Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names,'' Vol. 44: Oita Prefecture. Kadokawa Shoten, January 1980, p. 829</ref>
The site where Taira no Kiyotsune drowned is believed to be near the Yakkan River estuary in present-day Yanagigaura, Usa City. A monument called ''Komatsuzuka'' (a [[gorintō]] and memorial stone) stands near the estuary. The name "Komatsu" derives from Kiyotsune's father, Taira no Shigemori, who was called ''Komatsu-dono.'' Additionally, the bridge closest to the Yakkan River mouth is named ''Komatsu Bridge.''
== Notable Events == * [[August 29, 2024]] – [[Typhoon No. 10 of the Reiwa 6 Era]] caused heavy rainfall, leading the Yakkan River to exceed the flood danger level at the Beppu Bridge monitoring station. Authorities issued flood danger information.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=Flood Warning for Yakkan River in Usa City (9:15) |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yakkan River]][[ja:駅館川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yakkan River]][[ja:駅館川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yakkan River]][[ja:駅館川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668226]] Wikidata name: Yakkan River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox River | name = Yakkan River | image = [[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] | caption = Downstream view from Yakkan Bridge | river_system_class = [[Secondary river system]] | river_system = Yakkan River | river_type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 43<ref>[https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html Introduction to Major Projects: Rivers, Erosion Control, Ports, etc.] Oita Prefecture Usa Civil Engineering Office, June 1, 2016</ref> | elevation = -- | discharge = | monitoring_station = | basin_area = 390<ref>''[https://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html Oita Clear Water Environment Conservation Policy]'' {{PDFLink|[https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf Chapter 2: Current Conditions and Challenges Surrounding the Water Environment]}} Oita Prefecture, March 2006</ref> | source = | mouth = [[Suo Sea]] (Usa City, Oita Prefecture) | region = [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]] | notes = | references = }}
'''Yakkan River''' (駅館川, ''Yakkan-gawa'') is a river in [[Usa City]], [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Japan, flowing into the [[Suo Sea]] as the main stream of the [[secondary river system]] Yakkan River.
== Geography ==
The Yakkan River begins where its tributaries, Tsufu River and Era River, which originate from [[:en:Mount Yufu|Mount Yufu]] and the [[Hiji Plateau]], converge near Kosaka in [[Usa City|Innai Town]], close to the [[:en:Usa-Beppu Road|Usa-Beppu Road]]'s [[Innai Interchange]]. The river flows from south to north through Usa City, emptying into the Suo Sea. Near the mouth, it forms the alluvial plain known as the [[Usa Plain]].
The Yakkan River system is known for its many [[:en:arch bridges|arch bridges]], and its tributaries include notable waterfalls like [[Higashi Shiiya Falls]] and [[Nishi Shiiya Falls]].
According to the ''Dazai Kannaishi'' (1841), "The Usa station was located beside the Usa River, hence it was called Yakkan River." This suggests the name originates from an ancient [[ekiya]] (travel station) where messengers to [[Usa Hachiman Shrine]] rested.<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 Japan Historical Place Names Journal, Vol. 92: Yakkan River]</ref> Before the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]], it was also known as Usa River or Tosa River.<ref>Editorial Committee for the ''Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names,'' Vol. 44: Oita Prefecture. Kadokawa Shoten, January 1980, p. 829</ref>
The site where Taira no Kiyotsune drowned is believed to be near the Yakkan River estuary in present-day Yanagigaura, Usa City. A monument called ''Komatsuzuka'' (a [[gorintō]] and memorial stone) stands near the estuary. The name "Komatsu" derives from Kiyotsune's father, Taira no Shigemori, who was called ''Komatsu-dono.'' Additionally, the bridge closest to the Yakkan River mouth is named ''Komatsu Bridge.''
== Notable Events == * [[August 29, 2024]] – [[Typhoon No. 10 of the Reiwa 6 Era]] caused heavy rainfall, leading the Yakkan River to exceed the flood danger level at the Beppu Bridge monitoring station. Authorities issued flood danger information.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=Flood Warning for Yakkan River in Usa City (9:15) |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yakkan River]][[ja:駅館川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yakkan River]][[ja:駅館川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yakkan River]][[ja:駅館川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668226]] Wikidata name: Yakkan River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox River | name = Yakkan River | image = [[File:Yakkan River in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG|300px]] | caption = Downstream view from Yakkan Bridge | river_system_class = [[Secondary river system]] | river_system = Yakkan River | river_type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 43<ref>[https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/usa17012/kasen-sabou-gaiyou.html Introduction to Major Projects: Rivers, Erosion Control, Ports, etc.] Oita Prefecture Usa Civil Engineering Office, June 1, 2016</ref> | elevation = -- | discharge = | monitoring_station = | basin_area = 390<ref>''[https://www.pref.oita.jp/soshiki/13350/kiyoraka.html Oita Clear Water Environment Conservation Policy]'' {{PDFLink|[https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/life/5529_5708_misc.pdf Chapter 2: Current Conditions and Challenges Surrounding the Water Environment]}} Oita Prefecture, March 2006</ref> | source = | mouth = [[Suo Sea]] (Usa City, Oita Prefecture) | region = [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]] | notes = | references = }}
'''Yakkan River''' (駅館川, ''Yakkan-gawa'') is a river in [[Usa City]], [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Japan, flowing into the [[Suo Sea]] as the main stream of the [[secondary river system]] Yakkan River.
== Geography ==
The Yakkan River begins where its tributaries, Tsufu River and Era River, which originate from [[:en:Mount Yufu|Mount Yufu]] and the [[Hiji Plateau]], converge near Kosaka in [[Usa City|Innai Town]], close to the [[:en:Usa-Beppu Road|Usa-Beppu Road]]'s [[Innai Interchange]]. The river flows from south to north through Usa City, emptying into the Suo Sea. Near the mouth, it forms the alluvial plain known as the [[Usa Plain]].
The Yakkan River system is known for its many [[:en:arch bridges|arch bridges]], and its tributaries include notable waterfalls like [[Higashi Shiiya Falls]] and [[Nishi Shiiya Falls]].
According to the ''Dazai Kannaishi'' (1841), "The Usa station was located beside the Usa River, hence it was called Yakkan River." This suggests the name originates from an ancient [[ekiya]] (travel station) where messengers to [[Usa Hachiman Shrine]] rested.<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 Japan Historical Place Names Journal, Vol. 92: Yakkan River]</ref> Before the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]], it was also known as Usa River or Tosa River.<ref>Editorial Committee for the ''Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names,'' Vol. 44: Oita Prefecture. Kadokawa Shoten, January 1980, p. 829</ref>
The site where Taira no Kiyotsune drowned is believed to be near the Yakkan River estuary in present-day Yanagigaura, Usa City. A monument called ''Komatsuzuka'' (a [[gorintō]] and memorial stone) stands near the estuary. The name "Komatsu" derives from Kiyotsune's father, Taira no Shigemori, who was called ''Komatsu-dono.'' Additionally, the bridge closest to the Yakkan River mouth is named ''Komatsu Bridge.''
== Notable Events == * [[August 29, 2024]] – [[Typhoon No. 10 of the Reiwa 6 Era]] caused heavy rainfall, leading the Yakkan River to exceed the flood danger level at the Beppu Bridge monitoring station. Authorities issued flood danger information.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=Flood Warning for Yakkan River in Usa City (9:15) |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>

The Yakkan River system is known for its many [[:en:arch bridges|arch bridges]], and its tributaries include notable waterfalls like [[Higashi Shiiya Falls]] and [[Nishi Shiiya Falls]].

This suggests the name originates from an ancient [[ekiya]] (travel station) where messengers to [[Usa Hachiman Shrine]] rested.<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 Japan Historical Place Names Journal, Vol. 92: Yakkan River]</ref> Before the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]], it was also known as Usa River or Tosa River.<ref>Editorial Committee for the ''Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names,'' Vol. 44: Oita Prefecture. Kadokawa Shoten, January 1980, p. 829</ref>
The site where Taira no Kiyotsune drowned is believed to be near the Yakkan River estuary in present-day Yanagigaura, Usa City. A monument called ''Komatsuzuka'' (a [[gorintō]] and memorial stone) stands near the estuary. The name "Komatsu" derives from Kiyotsune's father, Taira no Shigemori, who was called ''Komatsu-dono.'' Additionally, the bridge closest to the Yakkan River mouth is named ''Komatsu Bridge.''
== Notable Events == * [[August 29, 2024]] – [[Typhoon No. 10 of the Reiwa 6 Era]] caused heavy rainfall, leading the Yakkan River to exceed the flood danger level at the Beppu Bridge monitoring station. Authorities issued flood danger information.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=Flood Warning for Yakkan River in Usa City (9:15) |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>

'''Yakkan River''' (駅館川, ''Yakkan-gawa'') is a river in [[Usa City]], [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Japan, flowing into the [[Suo Sea]] as the main stream of the [[secondary river system]] Yakkan River.
== Geography ==
The Yakkan River begins where its tributaries, Tsufu River and Era River, which originate from {{ill|Mount Yufu|en|Mount Yufu|WD=Q705288|lt=Mount Yufu}} and the [[Hiji Plateau]], converge near Kosaka in [[Usa City|Innai Town]], close to the {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|en|Usa-Beppu Road|WD=Q2743567|lt=Usa-Beppu Road}}'s [[Innai Interchange]]. The river flows from south to north through Usa City, emptying into the Suo Sea. Near the mouth, it forms the alluvial plain known as the [[Usa Plain]].
The Yakkan River system is known for its many [[:en:arch bridges|arch bridges]], and its tributaries include notable waterfalls like [[Higashi Shiiya Falls]] and [[Nishi Shiiya Falls]].
According to the ''Dazai Kannaishi'' (1841), "The Usa station was located beside the Usa River, hence it was called Yakkan River." This suggests the name originates from an ancient [[ekiya]] (travel station) where messengers to [[Usa Hachiman Shrine]] rested.<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 Japan Historical Place Names Journal, Vol. 92: Yakkan River]</ref> Before the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]], it was also known as Usa River or Tosa River.<ref>Editorial Committee for the ''Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names,'' Vol. 44: Oita Prefecture. Kadokawa Shoten, January 1980, p. 829</ref>
The site where Taira no Kiyotsune drowned is believed to be near the Yakkan River estuary in present-day Yanagigaura, Usa City. A monument called ''Komatsuzuka'' (a [[gorintō]] and memorial stone) stands near the estuary. The name "Komatsu" derives from Kiyotsune's father, Taira no Shigemori, who was called ''Komatsu-dono.'' Additionally, the bridge closest to the Yakkan River mouth is named ''Komatsu Bridge.''
== Notable Events == * [[August 29, 2024]] – [[Typhoon No. 10 of the Reiwa 6 Era]] caused heavy rainfall, leading the Yakkan River to exceed the flood danger level at the Beppu Bridge monitoring station. Authorities issued flood danger information.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=Flood Warning for Yakkan River in Usa City (9:15) |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>

'''Yakkan River''' (駅館川, ''Yakkan-gawa'') is a river in [[Usa City]], [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Japan, flowing into the [[Suo Sea]] as the main stream of the [[secondary river system]] Yakkan River.
== Geography ==
The Yakkan River begins where its tributaries, Tsufu River and Era River, which originate from {{ill|Mount Yufu|en|Mount Yufu|WD=Q705288|lt=Mount Yufu|1=Mount Yufu}} and the [[Hiji Plateau]], converge near Kosaka in [[Usa City|Innai Town]], close to the {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|en|Usa-Beppu Road|WD=Q2743567|lt=Usa-Beppu Road|1=Usa-Beppu Road}}'s [[Innai Interchange]]. The river flows from south to north through Usa City, emptying into the Suo Sea. Near the mouth, it forms the alluvial plain known as the [[Usa Plain]].
The Yakkan River system is known for its many [[:en:arch bridges|arch bridges]], and its tributaries include notable waterfalls like [[Higashi Shiiya Falls]] and [[Nishi Shiiya Falls]].
According to the ''Dazai Kannaishi'' (1841), "The Usa station was located beside the Usa River, hence it was called Yakkan River." This suggests the name originates from an ancient [[ekiya]] (travel station) where messengers to [[Usa Hachiman Shrine]] rested.<ref>[https://japanknowledge.com/articles/blogjournal/howtoread/entry.html?entryid=101 Japan Historical Place Names Journal, Vol. 92: Yakkan River]</ref> Before the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]], it was also known as Usa River or Tosa River.<ref>Editorial Committee for the ''Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names,'' Vol. 44: Oita Prefecture. Kadokawa Shoten, January 1980, p. 829</ref>
The site where Taira no Kiyotsune drowned is believed to be near the Yakkan River estuary in present-day Yanagigaura, Usa City. A monument called ''Komatsuzuka'' (a [[gorintō]] and memorial stone) stands near the estuary. The name "Komatsu" derives from Kiyotsune's father, Taira no Shigemori, who was called ''Komatsu-dono.'' Additionally, the bridge closest to the Yakkan River mouth is named ''Komatsu Bridge.''
== Notable Events == * [[August 29, 2024]] – [[Typhoon No. 10 of the Reiwa 6 Era]] caused heavy rainfall, leading the Yakkan River to exceed the flood danger level at the Beppu Bridge monitoring station. Authorities issued flood danger information.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/oita/20240829/5070019348.html |title=Flood Warning for Yakkan River in Usa City (9:15) |publisher=NHK |date=2024-08-29 |accessdate=2024-08-29}}</ref>
